title: coleus ciliatus
intro: Coleus ciliatus, also known as Painted Nettle, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive leaves and purple flowers. Traditionally, the leaves of Coleus ciliatus are used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to treat wounds. Herbal preparations of Coleus ciliatus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional Indian and Ayurvedic medicine.
